title: feat: prune schema-level template artifacts and non-PRD entity seeds
motivation: Schema seeds and queue views still include template-centric entities and worker categories that are outside PRD, increasing drift and migration risk.
behaviour:
Introduce safe migration steps to retire template entity seeds and queue views not used by PRD workflows.

Code examples:

```sql
-- template seeds to retire
INSERT INTO entity_types (type, schema) VALUES
  ('task', '{}'),
  ('tag', '{}'),
  ('channel', '{}'),
  ('message', '{}');
```

```sql
DROP VIEW IF EXISTS task_queue_view_coding;
DROP VIEW IF EXISTS task_queue_view_analysis;
DROP VIEW IF EXISTS task_queue_view_code_cleanup;
```

in: Add migration audit/checkpoint steps before destructive schema removal.
Remove non-PRD entity type seeds and template-only task queue views.
Update db tests and docs to reflect PRD-aligned schema surface.
out: Changes to PRD-required schema tables and policy triggers.
Cross-service refactors unrelated to schema cleanup.

acceptance_criteria: Schema no longer seeds non-PRD template entity types.
Template-only queue views are removed with safe migration sequencing.
DB docs/tests reflect the cleaned PRD-aligned schema shape.
test_plan: Run DB migration and schema tests on a fresh database and an existing upgraded database.
Verify no application path references removed views/entity seeds after migration.
Run integration smoke tests for wiki/transcription/campaign/compliance flows post-migration.
